name: Cayenne Pepper Wafers
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Cream together the Gruyere cheese and softened butter.
3. In a separate bowl, sift together the Cayenne pepper, salt, and all-purpose flour.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture, stirring until well combined.
5. Add the chopped walnuts or pecans and mix until evenly distributed.
6. Divide the dough into two portions. Shape each portion into logs that are 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
7. Wrap the logs tightly and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
8. Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
9. Using a sharp knife, cut the chilled logs into slices that are 1/4 inch thick.
10. Arrange the wafer slices on the prepared baking sheets, spacing them 1 to 2 inches apart.
11. Bake in the preheated oven for 15 to 20 minutes, or until lightly colored and crisp.
12. Remove from the oven and let cool before storing in an airtight container.
Makes 6 dozen.
